2603.15404.title: Detection of Autonomous Shuttles in Urban Traffic Images Using Adaptive Residual Context
2603.15404.paper_summary: The progressive automation of transport promises to enhance safety and sustainability through shared mobility. Like other vehicles and road users, and even more so for such a new technology, it requires monitoring to understand how it interacts in traffic and to evaluate its safety. This can be done with fixed cameras and video object detection. However, the addition of new detection targets generally requires a fine-tuning approach for regular detection methods. Unfortunately, this implementation strategy will lead to a phenomenon known as catastrophic forgetting, which causes a degradation in scene understanding. In road safety applications, preserving contextual scene knowledge is of the utmost importance for protecting road users. We introduce the Adaptive Residual Context (ARC) architecture to address this. ARC links a frozen context branch and trainable task-specific branches through a Context-Guided Bridge, utilizing attention to transfer spatial features while preserving pre-trained representations. Experiments on a custom dataset show that ARC matches fine-tuned baselines while significantly improving knowledge retention, offering a data-efficient solution to add new vehicle categories for complex urban environments.
2603.15404.paper_summary_zh: 運輸的逐步自動化承諾通過共享移動性來增強安全性和可持續性。像其他車輛和道路使用者一樣，尤其是對於這樣一項新技術，它需要監控以了解其在交通中的互動並評估其安全性。這可以通過固定攝像頭和視頻物體檢測來實現。然而，新增的檢測目標通常需要對常規檢測方法進行微調。遺憾的是，這種實施策略會導致一種稱為災難性遺忘的現象，這會導致場景理解的退化。在道路安全應用中，保留上下文場景知識對於保護道路使用者至關重要。我們引入了自適應殘差上下文（ARC）架構來解決這個問題。ARC通過上下文引導橋連接一個凍結的上下文分支和可訓練的任務特定分支，利用注意力來轉移空間特徵，同時保留預訓練的表示。在一個自定義數據集上的實驗顯示，ARC與微調的基準相匹配，同時顯著提高知識保留，為在複雜城市環境中添加新車輛類別提供了一種數據高效的解決方案。
2603.15404.author: Mohamed Aziz Younes et.al.
2603.15404.authors: Mohamed Aziz Younes, Nicolas Saunier, Guillaume-Alexandre Bilodeau
